rulings.Case Title: EST. OF DER HONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Appointment Of Receiver Upon Court Acceptance Of Resignation Of Executor [California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 564(B)(9)]
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES: 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) Issue in PES-16-299969 Paul Perdue as attorney for Jack Jair alleged a supplemental I&A was filed showing cash of $2,039,727.47. No date for the filing was provided. No Supplemental I& has been filed. 3) Issue in PES-16-299969 Paul Perdue as attorney for Jack Jair has not provided the required proposed order for the status petition. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [See LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Case Title: RE SOCORRO S. ARTATES -TRUST 1992
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Concerning Internal Affairs Of The Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Matter set for hearing for the court to determine how to proceed. 2) Petitioner requests a. Petitioners be confirmed as trustee. b. Respondents account from August 1, 1999 through present. c. John Artates pay market rate for his tenancy at Chenery Street Property. d. Trustees include the issue of all of settlor's deceased children as income distributes. e. The agreement of at least 4 of settler's children or issue of deceased children be required regarding management of Chenery Street. f. Trust Estate be reimbursed for any expenditures found to be improper or income which cannot be properly accounted for.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF VERONICA MARZOLF
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/10/21 for CIU report to be completed and proof of payment of the assessment fee to be filed, if ordered. Additionally, the conservator must address why she billed again for preparing paperwork for the move to Hayes Convalescent Hospital, when that moved occurred in 2018, and to facilitate transition to skilled nursing facility, when such services to the person were already also billed in the last accounting period. The total hours for services to the person also appears to be incorrect, although the lower number of hours shown in the petition may be appropriate if the services should not have included services facilitating the transition to the SNF. When was the bond fee paid on 4/30/20 ordered? No calculation was shown.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HELGA MOLDENHAUER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/2/2021. 1) Examiner has not received the required courtesy copy of the amended petition or supplement filed. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2) Please provide an updated proposed order for the amended petition. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 3) See CRC 7.53. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/18/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLAUDE L HEATER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Account ends 03/05/2021. I&A filed 01/21/2021 alleges $952,926.17 is all in Wells Fargo Claude L. Heater estate account no #7468526764. Letter from Wells Fargo indicates the account #6764 was opened 9/15/2020. Unclear why the I&A has this account when it was not opened until 9/15/2020 and Decedent died 05/28/2020. Appears I&A must be re-done with accounts in existence when Decedent died. 2) Bank statement filed 7/19/2021 do not verify the balance at the end of the account period on 03/05/2021. [See LRSF 14.35 D.] Bank Letter dated 07/12/2021 alleges the current balance is $932,735.25. 06/30/2021 Bank Statement alleges balance on 04/01/2021 to 06/30/2021. 3) Examiner received unfiled bank statement. [See LRSF 14.7 C.] The statement has been left at window 202 for counsel to pick up no later than 10/11/2021 or the statement will be recycled. 4) Examiner sent notes regarding the estate keeping assets over $250,000.00 in a single account. In the 08/19/2021 Amended Argument Why FDIC Insurance Is Not Required Petitioners cites unpublished cases and "California Rules of Court 81115(b)(1)." 5) John Pollak was reimbursed $65.00 for parking. [See LRSF 14.58 B. Absorbed as part of fees.] 6) John Pollak was reimbursed $320.00 for E-filing costs Lightning Legal. No receipts provided. Usual court filing fee is $5.00/$10.00 per filing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARK CHRISTOPHER DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. The administrator is authorized and directed to pay Richard Burger, his attorney, $34,698, as statutory fees for services to the administrator and to the estate. The administrator is authorized and directed to reimburse Richard Burger $1,573.75 for costs advanced. The administrator is authorized to retain a reserve in the amount of $5,000.00. Any unexpended portion of the such reserve shall be distributed equally 1/2 to Kurt Davis and 1/2 to Craig Davis without further order of this court. The following shall be distributed to craig davis 2011 hundai sonata, cash in the amount of $1,111,607.96. The following shall be distributed to Kurt Davis cash in the amount of $1,116,607.96.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTOPHER CARDENAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/26/2021. 1) Please explain how the calculation of statory compensation complies with CRC 7.705. It appears in the 9/10/2021 amendment to amended petition the attorney has included the interest twice in the calculation and calculation of statutory compensation does not appear correct. 2) Please provide a proposed order that includes the updated calculations and updated requests. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/11/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YAMILET DEL CARMEN GUTIERREZ-GONZALEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/26/2021. 1) Examiner requested a legal basis for whether guardian ad litem needs to be appointed for the minor heirs. No response filed. Instead 2 Ex Parte petitions for appointment filed. Petitioner has not complied with LRSF 14.11. 4) Please provide a legal basis for the request for $10,000.00 in bond. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/11/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JAIR 1988 FAMILY TRUST AKA THE RAYMOND JAIR TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES: 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) Issue in PES-16-299969 Paul Perdue as attorney for Jack Jair alleged a supplemental I&A was filed showing cash of $2,039,727.47. No date for the filing was provided. No Supplemental I& has been filed. 3) Issue in PES-16-299969 Paul Perdue as attorney for Jack Jair has not provided the required proposed order for the status petition. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [See LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DIANA IGNATIUS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate is set for 10/12/2022. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 10/12/2022. Appearance will be required on 10/12/2022 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE CLAUDE ESMIOL REVOCABLE TRUST DATED NOVEMBER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Assets Of Trust And For Order Transferring Assets To Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. Issues: 1) This Court ordinarily requires a copy of the bank statement immediately preceding a settlor's date of death in order to confirm that title to the account was vested in the name of the settlor, individually. No bank statements have been filed and only one of the accounts was identified on the schedule of trust assets. 2) The petitioners allege that the operating agreement for Esmiol Properties, LLC, allows a member to transfer his or her interests to a trust, but no copy of the operating agreement was submitted. 3) Notice was not personally served on Wells Fargo or Esmiol Properties. [Prob. C. Sects. 851 and 17203 and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] Additionally, notice was not mailed to any individual authorized to represent either organization, merely mailed to the organizations. The Court to determine if notice was sufficient. If the petition is supplemented with the supporting documents, the Court to determine if notice of the supplement should be given or waived. [CRC 7.53(b).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TINCHOR GEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/26/2021. 1) Please address LRSF 14.26 A. and LRSF 14.26 B. 2) Please file evidence showing the assets are community property. 3) Please file account statements for the alleged assets indicating how the assets are held and the amount. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/11/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
